retrieved_docs.title: Operative Risk -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Preoperative modification of the risk factors is the most effective way to prevent postoperative morbidity and mortality. Appropriate postoperative care can also diminish operative risk if contemplated before surgical treatment. After surgery, admission to a critical care unit may prevent postoperative complications more effectively than escalating postoperative care once complications develop. [5] Of note, the operative risk is the risk of both immediate and long-term adverse events. For example, evidence-based prevention of venous thromboembolism after surgical treatment of cancer often implies chemoprophylaxis that extends beyond discharge from the hospital. [33]

retrieved_docs.title: Posttransplantation Cancer -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer is one of the three major causes of death after an organ transplant. Cardiovascular disease and infection are the other two reasons for death after an organ transplant, but they both are decreasing in frequency because of effective screening, prophylaxis, and interventional therapies. Knowledge of post-transplant malignancy is inadequate regarding early detection and lack of established guidelines. In this activity, we focus on the epidemiology, pathophysiology, presentation, diagnosis, screening and highlight the role of the interprofessional team in its treatment strategy.

retrieved_docs.title: Breast Cancer Surgery -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Breast cancer is the most common cancer in women in the United States with constantly evolving treatment guidelines. The treatment of breast cancer often involves surgery, including breast conservation surgery or mastectomy. This activity outlines the surgical management of breast cancer and reviews the role of the interprofessional team in evaluating and treating patients who undergo surgery for breast cancer.

retrieved_docs.title: Breast Cancer Conservation Therapy -- Nursing, Allied Health, and Interprofessional Team Interventions
retrieved_docs.content: Secondly, it is crucial to provide thorough education surrounding the operation. Although surgeons do review the procedure and postoperative restrictions, many patients may be overwhelmed at that time. This stress may inhibit the patient, and their family, from thoroughly comprehending the information. A personal, one-on-one conversation can ensure the patient understands the operation. Reviewing the wire or wireless localization is necessary as many patients have a biopsy marker and may not understand why the area needs to be localized. The clinician should also review the purpose of sentinel lymph node biopsy, as many patients misconstrue this, assuming cancer has spread to the lymph nodes.

retrieved_docs.title: Breast Cancer Surgery -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Breast cancer involves an interprofessional team to achieve the best possible outcomes. This team includes oncologic and plastic surgeons, medical oncology, radiation oncology, pathology, radiology, nurse navigators, and multiple other individuals to discuss each patient and formulate a treatment plan. Oncology specialty nursing staff will assist during procedures, provide post-procedural follow-up care, and administer medication, and answer patient questions. These nurses must keep the clinician staff up to speed on all developments for the patient's progress or lack thereof. In cases where chemotherapy will be an adjunct, a board-certified oncology pharmacist should work with the oncology team to select the appropriate agents, verify dosing, and counsel the patient on adverse effects, also reporting any concerns to the team. The outcomes for patients with breast cancer continue to improve with the increased use of interprofessional teams, as demonstrated in multiple retrospective studies.

retrieved_docs.title: Breast Cancer Conservation Therapy -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify Implthe indications for breast conservation therapy in the management of early-stage breast cancer. Determine the preoperative workup, operative steps, and post-operative follow-up for partial mastectomy with axillary lymph node mapping. Identify the most common adverse events following breast conservation therapy and associated risk factors. Implement interprofessional team strategies for improving care coordination and communication to advance breast-conserving therapy and improve outcomes. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Evidence-Based Medicine -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Finally, clinicians using evidence-based medicine must put all of the information in the context of the patient's values or preferences. The patient's values or preferences may conflict with some of the possible options. Even strong evidence supporting a specific treatment may not be compatible with the patient's preferences, and thus, the clinician may not recommend the treatment to the patient. Also, the treatment might not apply to the specific patient. As an example, a patient may have a particular form of cancer. Level IA evidence may suggest life expectancy can double from 8 to 16 months with chemotherapy. Chemotherapy has significant side effects. The patient may find those side effects not acceptable and elect not to pursue chemotherapy secondary to the specific patient's preferences and values.

retrieved_docs.title: Tumor Treatment Fields in Oncology -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Tumor treatment fields (TTF) are a recent addition to the arsenal in our fight against cancer and have shown promising initial results. Unlike other cancer treatment modalities, it is associated with minimal side effects. This activity reviews the mechanism of action and the role of TTF against various neoplastic conditions and highlights the role of the interprofessional team in the management.

retrieved_docs.title: Ultrasound Therapy -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Numerous studies have shown level II and level III evidence for MRgFUS that demonstrate its safety and effectiveness for breast and uterine soft tissue masses. A systematic review conducted by Peek Et al. found the absence of a tumor or residual tumor in 95.8% of patients undergoing HIFU treatment for breast cancer. [17] Typically, breast cancer receives treatment with breast-conserving surgery or mastectomy. However, these procedures suffer from an increased risk of bleeding, infection, pain, and cosmetic issues. Thus, the need for alternative therapies, like HIFU, has increased. Additionally, some patients with significant co-morbidities may not be surgical candidates, or the surgery may. Hysterectomy can be curative for uterine fibroids but should only be an option for women who are no longer interested in bearing children. [18] Stewart Et al. found that 71% of women experienced symptom reduction at six months with a low incidence of adverse events. [19] Though no longitudinal studies examining the long-term effects of HIFU exist, Bohlmann Et al. reviewed 100 cases and found no increased risk of miscarriage or other obstetrical outcomes. [20] FDA approval of MRgFUS for uterine fibroids occurred in 2004, and numerous studies have demonstrated its effectiveness and safety

retrieved_docs.title: Rectal Cancer Microsurgery -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Colorectal malignancies are considered the third most common cancer for both male and female individuals. Moreover, they are the second most common cause of malignancy-related mortalities in the United States. Rectal microsurgery is an emerging technique that offers an oncologic cure without compromising bowel function. As the repertoire of rectal surgery expands, a good overall understanding of new procedures and platforms is needed to ensure optimal patient selection. This activity reviews the indications, techniques, platforms, and complications of rectal microsurgery and the role of the interprofessional team in the workup and postoperative care of these patients.

retrieved_docs.title: Breast Cancer Surgery -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Breast cancer often requires surgery as part of curative treatment. The decision to proceed with mastectomy or breast conservation surgery remains both patient- and disease-driven. Some patients require upfront chemotherapy and/or radiation treatment to downstage their tumor or axillary nodes, as is the case in inflammatory breast cancer. In most early-stage breast cancer, surgery is the first step in treatment. Following surgery, adjuvant radiation is recommended in nearly all patients who undergo breast conservation therapy as recurrence rates are unacceptably high without it. Endocrine therapy is recommended for at least five years in those whose tumors are positive for hormone receptors (ie, estrogen, progesterone) and is often recommended for women considered high risk as prophylactic therapy. Chemotherapy is also recommended in more aggressive tumors as well as those that have a negative expression of estrogen, progesterone, and HER2neu receptors. Surveillance guidelines following cure involve at least annual mammography to assess for local recurrence or contralateral breast cancers. The timing of initiation is generally six months after radiation therapy is completed. If the patient is receiving semiannual screening, current guidelines recommend returning to annual surveillance after 2 years. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 4 -- Community 2760
retrieved_docs.content: إزالة المبيض جزئيًا (partial oophorectomy) and استئصال الرحم جزئيًا (partial hysterectomy) are gynecologic surgical procedures that frequently co‑occur, often being performed together in the management of conditions requiring simultaneous partial removal of ovarian and uterine tissue. This co‑occurrence reflects a clinical approach where both organs are addressed in a single operative session to treat disease, reduce patient morbidity, and streamline postoperative recovery.

Overall, these interrelated procedures illustrate a structured taxonomy of uterine, cervical, and ovarian surgeries, their co‑occurrence patterns, and their inclusion in a unified classification of malignant‑disease‑related therapeutic and diagnostic interventions, which is essential for accurate coding, clinical decision‑making, and comprehensive patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2173
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer of thyroid is classified as a subtype of اضطراب صماوي (endocrine disorder) and is clinically associated with استئصال الدرقية (thyroidectomy), reflecting its categorization within endocrine pathologies and the common surgical intervention used in its treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2411
retrieved_docs.content: Partial removal of the ovary (إزالة المبيض جزئيًا) and partial removal of the uterus (استئصال الرحم جزئيًا) are surgical procedures that frequently co‑occur in clinical practice. Similarly, bilateral removal of the fallopian tubes and ovaries (استئصال البوق والمبيض الثنائي) also commonly co‑occurs with partial hysterectomy (استئصال الرحم جزئيًا). The repeated association of these gynecologic surgeries indicates that, when extensive pelvic pathology or prophylactic indications are present, clinicians often combine ovarian or salpingo‑oophorectomy with a partial hysterectomy to address multiple reproductive organs in a single operative session, thereby streamlining patient care and reducing the need for separate procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 2 -- Community 1272
retrieved_docs.content: specific methods of exploration. Parathyroid exploration is captured by parathyroidectomy procedures, which may be performed alone or with mediastinal exploration via a cervical approach or a sternal split approach. Collectively, these diverse surgical techniques illustrate how the umbrella term “exploration procedure” integrates multiple organ‑specific operations, each with its own methodological variations and optional adjunctive steps, to facilitate diagnostic assessment, tumor removal, ductal clearance, or decompression in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 2 -- Community 933
retrieved_docs.content: The head and neck structure is identified as the anatomic site associated with cancer of the thyroid. Thyroid cancer is clinically associated with the procedure استئصال الدرقية (thyroidectomy). Thyroidectomy, whether performed as a partial or complete operation (thyroidectomy; partial or complete), is a targeted procedure for the head and neck structure and serves as the parent procedure for both استئصال الدرقية (full thyroidectomy) and استئصال الدرقية الجزئي (partial thyroidectomy). The partial thyroidectomy (استئصال الدرقية الجزئي) frequently co‑occurs with دراق, reflecting a contextual relationship. Understanding these interconnections is essential for planning appropriate surgical management of thyroid cancer within the head and neck region.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 4 -- Community 2873
retrieved_docs.content: The CTRP Intervention Terminology encompasses the surgical procedures استئصال قناة فالوب and استئصال المبيض. These two interventions frequently co‑occur, indicating that they are often performed together in clinical practice, such as in comprehensive gynecologic surgery. Inclusion of both concepts within the CTRP Intervention Terminology highlights their relevance as related operative interventions within the same therapeutic domain.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 4 -- Community 2833
retrieved_docs.content: Breast: excisions are classified as a subcategory of the broader surgical procedure mastectomy, and the term mastectomy is also expressed in Arabic as استئصال ثديي.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 4 -- Community 2863
retrieved_docs.content: Procedures on breast and breast: excisions are both classified as subcategories of mastectomy, indicating that mastectomy serves as the parent operation that encompasses these specific breast surgical interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1927
retrieved_docs.content: The female breast is the anatomical location where cancer of breast is identified, serving as the primary finding site for this malignancy. In the management of breast cancer, the female breast is also the target organ for breast‑conserving surgical procedures, specifically lumpectomy and quadrantectomy of breast, which aim to remove cancerous tissue while preserving as much normal breast tissue as possible.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1931
retrieved_docs.content: Head and Neck Surgery is a medical specialty that includes procedures such as بضع طبلة الأذن, which is classified as a type of Head and Neck Surgery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 2195
retrieved_docs.content: The female breast serves as the anatomical site where breast cancer manifests, with “cancer of breast” identified as a finding located in this tissue. A specific manifestation of this disease is the primary malignant neoplasm of the areola of the female breast, which is classified as a type of breast cancer. Therapeutic management of breast cancer frequently involves breast‑conserving surgical procedures performed on the female breast, such as lumpectomy and quadrantectomy, which are directly related to the organ and aim to remove malignant tissue while preserving as much normal breast tissue as possible. This interconnected knowledge underscores the relationship between the site (female breast), the disease (cancer of breast and its areolar subtype), and the standard surgical interventions (lumpectomy; quadrantectomy of breast) used in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 4 -- Community 2857
retrieved_docs.content: Procedures on breast are classified under the broader category of mastectomy, and the term mastectomy corresponds to the Arabic phrase استئصال ثديي, indicating that both refer to the surgical removal of breast tissue.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 897
retrieved_docs.content: The female breast is the anatomical site that undergoes both diagnostic and therapeutic procedures. Breast biopsy and other diagnostic interventions are performed on the female breast to obtain tissue samples and assess pathology. In addition, the female breast is the target of surgical treatments such as lumpectomy and quadrantectomy, which are procedures used to remove localized breast lesions while preserving as much normal tissue as possible. These relationships underscore the continuum of care for breast conditions, linking diagnostic evaluation with appropriate surgical management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 27 -- Level 4 -- Community 2888
retrieved_docs.content: CTRP Intervention Terminology includes the concept of استئصال المعدة, which denotes a surgical procedure involving partial or total removal of the stomach (gastric resection). By incorporating استئصال المعدة, the CTRP Intervention Terminology classifies this type of gastric surgery within its set of medical interventions, reflecting its relevance for documenting and managing patients who undergo stomach resection procedures.
